name: JD Vance
summary: JD Vance's character and pattern of behavior are marked by a consistent disregard for facts and a willingness to spread misinformation to further his political ambitions. He has a history of making derogatory comments about certain groups, including childless individuals, immigrants, and members of the LGBTQ+ community. Vance's nonprofit organization, Our Ohio Renewal, was found to have spent more on overhead costs and travel than on programs to address opioid abuse, and his charity work has been criticized for being a front for his political ambitions. He has also demonstrated a tendency to shift his opinions and alliances in pursuit of power, having previously criticized Donald Trump before becoming his running mate. Vance's behavior is characterized by a lack of empathy and a willingness to use divisive rhetoric to mobilize support for his political agenda.

entries.fact: JD Vance complained about 'highly educated transplants' in Silicon Valley in a New York Times op-ed, and said elite tech crowds wielded 'political-financial power in combination with a certain condescension' in an interview.

JD Vance's nonprofit Our Ohio Renewal spent more on overhead costs and travel than on programs to fight opioid abuse, with over $63,000 spent on 'management services' provided by its executive director, who later became Vance's chief campaign strategist.

Our Ohio Renewal's biggest accomplishment, sending psychiatrist Sally Satel to Ohio's Appalachian region for a yearlong residency, was 'tainted' by the ties among Satel, her employer, American Enterprise Institute (AEI), and Purdue Pharma, in the form of knowledge exchange between Satel and Purdue and financial support from Purdue to AEI.
JD Vance said in a podcast interview that being childless 'makes people more sociopathic and ultimately our whole country a little bit less, less mentally stable'

JD Vance said in a speech at a Center for Christian Virtue leadership meeting that childless teachers were 'trying to brainwash the minds of our children' and criticized American Federation of Teachers President Randi Weingarten
JD Vance suggested in an interview on The Charlie Kirk Show that childless people should be taxed at a higher rate than those with children

JD Vance said in a Fox News interview that the country is run by 'a bunch of childless cat ladies who are miserable at their own lives and the choices that they've made and so they want to make the rest of the country miserable too'

Tim Ryan, the Democratic nominee, said JD Vance's charity Our Ohio Renewal was a front for Vance's political ambitions, pointing to reports that the organization paid a Vance political adviser and conducted public opinion polling, while its efforts to address addiction failed.
JD Vance voted against raising the debt ceiling, standing against final passage of the Fiscal Responsibility Act of 2023.
JD Vance introduced legislation that would have made gender-affirming care for minors a federal crime, with penalties of up to 12 years in prison.
JD Vance sponsored the Dismantle DEI Act, which would ban federal diversity, equity, and inclusion programs and funding for agencies, contractors, and organizations receiving federal funds.
JD Vance was chosen by Donald Trump as his running mate in the 2024 presidential election
JD Vance accepted the nomination to be Donald Trump's running mate
A former friend of JD Vance's from Yale Law School alleged that Vance has changed his opinion on many issues in pursuit of political power and wealth
JD Vance said that a vice president 'doesn't really matter' and that Kamala Harris 'has been a bad vice president'
JD Vance told Kamala Harris to 'go to hell' after she criticized Donald Trump's visit to Arlington National Cemetery
JD Vance said that Donald Trump would veto a national abortion ban, but Trump later denied discussing the issue with Vance

JD Vance alleged that Haitian illegal immigrants were 'draining social services and generally causing chaos all over Springfield, Ohio' and that 'reports now show that people have had their pets abducted and eaten by people who shouldn't be in this country'

JD Vance promoted conservative activist Christopher Rufo's allegation that African migrants were eating cats in Dayton, Ohio, despite Dayton authorities reporting 'no evidence to even remotely suggest that any group, including our immigrant community, is engaged in eating pets'

JD Vance alleged a 'massive rise in communicable diseases' in Springfield, but Clark County's health commissioner reported having 'not seen a substantial increase in all reportable communicable diseases'
JD Vance used the death of a child in Springfield for political gain, despite the child's father criticizing him for doing so
JD Vance defended his claims about Haitian migrants eating cats, saying that he was willing to create stories to get the American media's attention
JD Vance said he did not believe Trump lost the 2020 presidential election and that he believed 'Big Tech rigged the election' through censorship
JD Vance objected to fact-checking during the vice presidential debate, arguing that the moderators should not have fact-checked his claims about Haitian immigrants in Springfield, Ohio
JD Vance cast the tie-breaking vote to confirm Pete Hegseth as Secretary of Defense
JD Vance wrote that judges aren't allowed to control the executive's legitimate power, after multiple federal judges issued temporary rulings against various Trump administration actions

JD Vance told Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy that it's disrespectful for him to litigate the conflict with Russia in front of the American media during a meeting in the White House's Oval Office
JD Vance incorrectly claimed that Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy campaigned for the opposition in Pennsylvania in October 2024
JD Vance visited Greenland and said that the US should acquire it, warning that Greenland faced the encroachment of powerful countries like China and Russia
JD Vance lamented that the globalist economy had caused the United States to borrow money from Chinese peasants to buy the things those Chinese peasants manufacture

JD Vance was an outspoken critic of Republican presidential nominee Donald Trump, calling him 'reprehensible' and himself a 'never Trump guy', though he later apologized for these criticisms and expressed support for Trump in 2021.

JD Vance was baptized and confirmed in the Catholic Church and has since drawn criticism from Pope Francis and his successor, Pope Leo XIV, for misrepresenting Church teaching in his support for policies like the deportation of migrants.

JD Vance said that if he had been vice president during the 2020 presidential election, he would not have certified the results and instead suggested some states Trump lost should have sent pro-Trump electors to Washington so that Congress could decide the election.
JD Vance advised Trump to fire all civil servants and replace them with Trump supporters.
JD Vance's anti-opioid nonprofit was found to have faltered, according to tax records.
JD Vance won the 2022 United States Senate Republican primary in Ohio with 32.22% of the vote.

JD Vance's cousin, Nate Vance, a U.S. Marine Corps veteran, volunteered to fight for Ukraine after Russia's invasion and later criticized JD Vance's stance on Ukraine, accusing him and Trump of aiding Russia.
JD Vance won the 2022 United States Senate election in Ohio with 53.04% of the vote.
Vance's anti-drug charity enlisted a doctor who echoed Big Pharma's views.
JD Vance made statements during a Senate debate that were criticized by the Ohio Democratic Party.
JD Vance argued that the country's largest and most powerful institutions have united against the right and called for 'a de-woke-ification program' even if the courts say it is illegal.
JD Vance voted against the bill to raise the debt ceiling
JD Vance opposed a bipartisan deal to avoid federal government default
JD Vance opposes abortion, same-sex marriage, and gun control, and has taken a number of natalist positions.
JD Vance introduced a measure banning DEI in the federal government
JD Vance was chosen by acclamation as Donald Trump's running mate in the 2024 presidential election.
JD Vance previously stated he was a 'never Trump' guy, found Trump 'reprehensible', and compared him to 'America's Hitler'
JD Vance made a comment about 'childless cat ladies' that sparked uproar from Taylor Swift fans and others
JD Vance defended his 'childless cat ladies' comment as 'sarcastic' amid blowback
JD Vance said that 'childless people' in US leadership are 'more sociopathic'
JD Vance proposed federal criminalization of gender-affirming care for minors.
JD Vance expressed a belief that childlessness is linked to sociopathy and suggested parents should have more voting power than non-parents, though he later backtracked from the latter suggestion.
JD Vance attacked AOC for promoting a 'sociopathic attitude' about children
Workers at a Kentucky startup funded by JD Vance alleged 'nightmare' working conditions.
JD Vance proposed dismantling federal DEI programs and banning 'X' gender passports
JD Vance told Kamala Harris to 'go to hell' for a nonexistent cemetery criticism
The future of JD Vance's charity in Ohio was unclear, according to The New York Times.
JD Vance had missed every U.S. Senate vote since becoming Donald Trump's vice presidential running mate
JD Vance accused Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy of showing a 'propaganda tour' of Ukraine and told him he should be thanking President Trump for trying to bring an end to the conflict
JD Vance called 'the threat from within' his biggest concern in terms of security for Europe, rather than Russia or China, during a speech at the Munich Security Conference
Vance argued for a higher tax rate on childless Americans in a 2021 interview
JD Vance said he regrets past criticism of Trump
J.D. Vance argued against the need for rape and incest exceptions in abortion laws.
J.D. Vance stated he would not support federal legislation to codify marriage equality and is against same-sex marriage.
JD Vance once described himself as a 'Never Trump guy' and used words like 'idiot' and 'Hitler' to describe Trump, but later became his vice-presidential nominee
JD Vance promoted far-right views in a speech about extremists' book
JD Vance finds a kindred spirit with Trump on election denial and Jan. 6
J.D. Vance proposed a federal ban on gender transition care for minors.
J.D. Vance once proposed a plan to give parents more votes in democracy than non-parents.
J.D. Vance has a history of disparaging people without kids
J.D. Vance endorsed a book that calls far-left individuals 'unhumans' and praises fascist dictators.
Vance bashed a teachers union president for not having children of her own
Vance pushed false accusations of Haitians eating pets
Vance spread debunked claims about Haitian immigrants eating pets
Vance urged followers to keep posting 'cat memes' despite his false claim about Haitian migrants
Vance shared a social media post about immigrants cooking pets in Dayton, which the city refuted
Vance stuck by his pet-eating claims and said he was willing to 'create stories'
JD Vance appeared at an event hosted by a hard-right Christian nationalist
JD Vance said that Trump didn't lose the 2020 election, using the phrase 'not by the words I would use'
Vice President JD Vance used the theological concept 'ordo amoris' to justify immigration policies, which was criticized by Pope Francis.

Pope Francis directly responded to Vice President JD Vance's use of the theological concept 'ordo amoris' to justify immigration policies, stating that the true ordo amoris is one of fraternity open to all, without exception, and that the administration's approach is built on force, not truth, and will end badly.
JD Vance declared ideological war on Europe in a speech at the Munich Security Conference
JD Vance sparked an extraordinary Oval Office skirmish by leaning hard into Trump's foreign policy
JD Vance told Greenlanders that their country is cold as 's---', and China's coming for them
JD Vance called Chinese people 'peasants', prompting China to lash out at him
A social media account bearing the name of Pope Leo XIV posted criticism of the Trump administration's immigration policy, directly calling out Vice President JD Vance.
JD Vance revealed something about himself that an expert considered ugly
JD Vance made a response to a controversial interview
JD Vance told attendees at TPUSA AmFest that they don't have to apologize for being white.
JD Vance said Americans don't have to apologize for being white anymore, sparking criticism for promoting hateful rhetoric.
JD Vance used racist tropes, prompting a response from Jasmine Crockett.
JD Vance sparked backlash after blaming an ICE shooting victim for her own death
JD Vance refused to apologize for claiming that Alex Pretti was an ICE assassin
